{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"event","params":{"type":"TurnBegin","payload":{"user_input":"You are an orchestrator for a **fan-out and synthesis** workflow.\n\nYour working directory is: /workspace\n\n## Rules\n- The task, required output format, save location, and decomposition guide are in your instruction above.\n- All input data files are in `/input_artifacts/`.\n- You have a Shell tool and a Task tool (to spawn sub-agents).\n- Sub-agents run in isolated contexts — include ALL necessary info in their prompts.\n\n## Workflow\n1. **Read your instruction** — understand the task, output requirements, and decomposition guide.\n2. **Fan-out phase**: Identify independent research/analysis threads. Spawn a sub-agent for each thread to work in parallel.\n3. **Collect**: Gather all sub-agent results.\n4. **Synthesis phase**: Integrate all perspectives into a unified output. Resolve contradictions, deduplicate findings, and produce a coherent answer.\n5. **Output**: Follow the output instructions exactly as specified in your task instruction.\n\n## Sub-Agent Instructions\n- Each sub-agent works on one independent thread/perspective.\n- They should NOT see each other's work — that's the point of independent analysis.\n- Spawn approximately 17 sub-agents for the fan-out phase.\n\n\nThe NetBox application at `/testbed` has sixteen bugs reported by users and maintainers. They are all independent of each other and scattered across different parts of the codebase. Fix all sixteen.\n\nBug 1 -- Image attachments overwrite each other on S3 storage\n\nUsers uploading image attachments via S3-compatible storage backends report that files with the same name silently overwrite each other instead of being deduplicated. The upload path logic must be refactored so that a helper function `_build_image_attachment_path()` constructs the deterministic path, and the main upload function calls Django's `Storage.get_available_name()` to guarantee collision avoidance regardless of which storage backend is active.\n\nBug 2 -- ScriptModule save triggers class synchronization twice\n\nSaving a ScriptModule triggers `sync_classes()` twice per save. Investigate the model's `save()` method and any signal handlers connected to ScriptModule's `post_save` signal to identify the redundant invocation, then eliminate it so synchronization happens exactly once.\n\nBug 3 -- humanize_speed filter shows wrong unit for non-round Gbps/Tbps\n\nThe `humanize_speed` template filter renders 2,500,000 Kbps as \"2500 Mbps\" instead of \"2.5 Gbps\" and 1,600,000,000 Kbps as \"1600 Gbps\" instead of \"1.6 Tbps\". The filter should always display speeds using the largest appropriate unit, including decimal values where the result is not a whole number.\n\nBug 4 -- Cable CSV bulk import fails for power feed terminations\n\nCSV bulk import of cables fails when one side terminates on a power feed. The cable import form assumes both sides belong to a device, but power feeds belong to power panels. The form needs `side_a_power_panel` and `side_b_power_panel` fields, and its side-cleaning logic must handle `powerfeed` content types by looking up the termination via the power panel rather than a device.\n\nBug 5 -- Interface speed field overflows above 2.1 Tbps\n\nLAG interfaces with aggregate speed above ~2.1 Tbps cannot be saved because the speed field on the Interface model is a 32-bit integer that overflows at 2,147,483,647. The model field must be widened to a 64-bit `PositiveBigIntegerField`, a corresponding database migration `0227_alter_interface_speed_bigint.py` must be created, all forms and filters that constrain the speed range must be updated to support the wider range (including adding a `MultiValueBigNumberFilter` to the utilities filters), and the GraphQL schema must use a matching BigInt type annotation.\n\nBug 6 -- Plugin template extensions missing on declarative-layout views\n\nPlugin template extensions registered via `PluginTemplateExtension` do not render on detail pages that were recently converted to declarative layout definitions. The affected views need `PluginContentPanel` slots added for left, right, and full-width positions. Investigate which IPAM and core views were recently migrated to declarative layouts and are now missing these panels.\n\nBug 7 -- Uploading a faulty script silently registers a broken module\n\nUploading a custom script file that contains syntax errors or import failures silently registers a broken module in the database, preventing re-upload due to a uniqueness constraint. A `validate_script_content()` function must be created that compiles and executes the uploaded content to verify it is loadable. Both the script file form's clean method and the script module API serializer's validate method must invoke this function before persisting the file.\n\nBug 8 -- Device-type YAML export omits port-mapping data\n\nWhen exporting device types or module types to YAML, the output is missing port-mapping entries. The port template mapping model needs a `to_yaml()` method, and the device type and module type YAML export methods must include port-mapping data in their output.\n\nBug 9 -- Interface CSV export renders connection column with whitespace artifacts\n\nCSV exports of interface tables render the connection column with multi-line template whitespace instead of clean values. The base path endpoint table class and the interface table both need a `value_connection()` method that produces clean CSV-friendly output, with the interface table handling virtual circuit connections specifically.\n\nBug 10 -- REST API accepts non-existent custom field names in PATCH requests\n\nPATCH requests to the REST API with custom field names that do not actually exist silently succeed, and the bogus names appear in changelogs. The custom fields data serializer field must validate incoming field names against the set of custom fields defined for the object type and raise `ValidationError` for unknown keys. Additionally, the base validated model serializer must preserve any normalization the model's `clean()` method performs on `custom_field_data`.\n\nBug 11 -- Module type import with port mappings fails on ambiguous port names\n\nImporting module types with port mappings fails with \"get() returned more than one\" errors when multiple types share similar front/rear port template names. The port template mapping import form needs methods to scope the front and rear port template querysets to the specific device type or module type being imported, preventing cross-type collisions.\n\nBug 12 -- Custom script \"last run\" shows creation time, not start time\n\nThe \"last run\" timestamp displayed for custom scripts shows the job's creation time instead of when it actually started executing. Find the mixin that computes the latest job ordering and fix it to order by the job's start time instead of its creation time.\n\nBug 13 -- API_TOKEN_PEPPERS rejects OrderedDict and other dict subclasses\n\nSetting `API_TOKEN_PEPPERS` to an `OrderedDict` or any other dict subclass raises `ImproperlyConfigured` even though the mapping is perfectly valid. The pepper validation function uses a strict type identity check instead of `isinstance`.\n\nBug 14 -- Script module Edit button hidden for non-superusers with correct permissions\n\nNon-superusers who have been granted the correct Django model permissions still cannot see the Edit button on the script module listing page. The template checks for a permission name that doesn't match Django's standard `change_scriptmodule` permission naming convention.\n\nBug 15 -- ColorField widget shows misleading help text about hex input\n\nA model field's `formfield()` method displays help text telling users to enter an RGB hex color manually, but the actual widget is a dropdown color picker that does not accept manual input. The misleading help text should be removed.\n\nBug 16 -- Contact group \"Contacts\" count wrong when a contact belongs to multiple nested groups\n\nThe contact group list view shows an inflated contact count when a contact is assigned to multiple child groups under the same parent. For example, if one contact is in both `Group A > Subgroup 1` and `Group A > Subgroup 2`, `Group A` counts that contact twice. The current annotation uses django-mptt's `add_related_count()` which cannot deduplicate across the ManyToMany relation. A custom manager with an `annotate_contacts()` method must replace the existing annotation calls. The detail view also double-counts contacts in a similar scenario and needs a `.distinct()` call on the contacts queryset.\n\nFix all sixteen bugs. Do not modify any files in the `tests/` directory. The repository is at `/testbed`.\n\n\n## Gold Decomposition Guide\ncoordination_pattern: fan-out-synthesize\n\nsub_tasks:\n  - id: fix_s3_duplicate_filenames\n    description: >\n      Fix image attachment uploads so filenames are always unique regardless\n      of storage backend. Remove the redundant post-save signal handler.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fix\n\n  - id: fix_humanize_speed_decimal\n    description: >\n      Update the humanize_speed template filter to always display speeds using\n      the largest appropriate unit, including decimal values where the result\n      is not a whole number (e.g. 2.5 Gbps, 1.6 Tbps).\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fix\n\n  - id: fix_cable_powerfeed_import\n    description: >\n      Fix cable CSV bulk import to handle power feed terminations that belong\n      to power panels instead of devices. Add power panel fields and update\n      the termination lookup logic to distinguish between device-based and\n      power-panel-based termination types.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fix\n\n  - id: fix_speed_overflow\n    description: >\n      Fix the interface speed field that overflows above 2.1 Tbps by widening it\n      from a 32-bit integer to a 64-bit integer. Add the missing PluginContentPanel slots to\n      views that were converted from template-based to declarative layouts.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fix\n\n  - id: fix_faulty_script_registration\n    description: >\n      Add validation to the script upload path so that files with import or\n      syntax errors are rejected before being persisted to the database. 299\tclass PathEndpoint(models.Model):\n   300\t    \"\"\"\n   301\t    An abstract model inherited by any CabledObjectModel subclass which represents the end of a CablePath; specifically,\n   302\t    these include ConsolePort, ConsoleServerPort, PowerPort, PowerOutlet, Interface, and PowerFeed.\n   303\t\n   304\t    `_path` references the CablePath originating from this instance, if any. It is set or cleared by the receivers in\n   305\t    dcim.signals in response to changes in the cable path, and complements the `origin` GenericForeignKey field on the\n   306\t    CablePath model. `_path` should not be accessed directly; rather, use the `path` property.\n   307\t\n   308\t    `connected_endpoints()` is a convenience method for returning the destination of the associated CablePath, if any.\n   309\t    \"\"\"\n   310\t\n   311\t    _path = models.ForeignKey(\n   312\t        to='dcim.CablePath',\n   313\t        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,\n   314\t        null=True,\n   315\t        blank=True,\n   316\t    )\n   317\t\n   318\t    class Meta:\n   319\t        abstract = True\n   320\t\n   321\t    def trace(self):\n   322\t        origin = self\n   323\t        path = []\n   324\t\n   325\t        # Construct the complete path (including e.g. bridged interfaces)\n   326\t        while origin is not None:\n   327\t            # Go through the public accessor rather than dereferencing `_path`\n   328\t            # directly. During cable edits, CablePath rows can be deleted and\n   329\t            # recreated while this endpoint instance is still in memory.\n   330\t            cable_path = origin.path\n   331\t            if cable_path is None:\n   332\t                break\n   333\t\n   334\t            path.extend(cable_path.path_objects)\n   335\t\n   336\t            # If the path ends at a non-connected pass-through port, pad out the link and far-end terminations\n   337\t            if len(path) % 3 == 1:\n   338\t                path.extend(([], []))\n   339\t            # If the path ends at a site or provider network, inject a null \"link\" to render an attachment\n   340\t            elif len(path) % 3 == 2:\n   341\t                path.insert(-1, [])\n   342\t\n   343\t            # Check for a bridged relationship to continue the trace.\n   344\t            destinations = cable_path.destinations\n   345\t            if len(destinations) == 1:\n   346\t                origin = getattr(destinations[0], 'bridge', None)\n   347\t            else:\n   348\t                origin = None\n   349\t\n   350\t        # Return the path as a list of three-tuples (A termination(s), cable(s), B termination(s))\n   351\t        return list(zip(*[iter(path)] * 3))\n   352\t\n   353\t    @property\n   354\t    def path(self):\n   355\t        \"\"\"\n   356\t        Return this endpoint's current CablePath, if any.\n   357\t\n   358\t        `_path` is a denormalized reference that is updated from CablePath\n   359\t        save/delete handlers, including queryset.update() calls on origin\n   360\t        endpoints. That means an already-instantiated endpoint can briefly hold\n   361\t        a stale in-memory `_path` relation while the database already points to\n   362\t        a different CablePath (or to no path at all).\n   363\t\n   364\t        If the cached relation points to a CablePath that has just been\n   365\t        deleted, refresh only the `_path` field from the database and retry.\n   366\t        This keeps the fix cheap and narrowly scoped to the denormalized FK.\n   367\t        \"\"\"\n   368\t        if self._path_id is None:\n   369\t            return None\n   370\t\n   371\t        try:\n   372\t            return self._path\n   373\t        except ObjectDoesNotExist:\n   374\t            # Refresh only the denormalized FK instead of the whole model.\n   375\t            # The expected problem here is in-memory staleness during path\n   376\t            # rebuilds, not persistent database corruption.\n   377\t            self.refresh_from_db(fields=['_path'])\n   378\t            return self._path if self._path_id else None\n   379\t\n   380\t    @cached_property\n   381\t    def connected_endpoints(self):\n   382\t        \"\"\"\n   383\t        Caching accessor for the attached CablePath's destinations (if any).\n   384\t\n   385\t        Always route through `path` so stale in-memory `_path` references are\n   386\t        repaired before we cache the result for the lifetime of this instance.\n   387\t        \"\"\"\n   388\t        if cable_path := self.path:\n   389\t            return cable_path.destinations\n   390\t        return []\n   391\t\n   392\t\n   393\t#\n   394\t# Console components\n   395\t#\n   396\t\n   397\tclass ConsolePort(ModularComponentModel, CabledObjectModel, PathEndpoint, TrackingModelMixin):\n   398\t    \"\"\"\n   399\t    A physical console port within a Device. For example, in the topology\n   502\t        below, PP1.get_downstream_powerports() would return PP2-4.\n   503\t\n   504\t               ---- PO1 <---> PP2\n   505\t             /\n   506\t        PP1 ------- PO2 <---> PP3\n   507\t             \\\n   508\t               ---- PO3 <---> PP4\n   509\t\n   510\t        \"\"\"\n   511\t        poweroutlets = self.poweroutlets.filter(cable__isnull=False)\n   512\t        if leg:\n   513\t            poweroutlets = poweroutlets.filter(feed_leg=leg)\n   514\t        if not poweroutlets:\n   515\t            return PowerPort.objects.none()\n   516\t\n   517\t        q = Q()\n   518\t        for poweroutlet in poweroutlets:\n   519\t            q |= Q(\n   520\t                cable=poweroutlet.cable,\n   521\t                cable_end=poweroutlet.opposite_cable_end\n   522\t            )\n   523\t\n   524\t        return PowerPort.objects.filter(q)\n   525\t\n   526\t    def get_power_draw(self):\n   527\t        \"\"\"\n   528\t        Return the allocated and maximum power draw (in VA) and child PowerOutlet count for this PowerPort.\n   529\t        \"\"\"\n   530\t        from dcim.models import PowerFeed\n   531\t\n   532\t        # Calculate aggregate draw of all child power outlets if no numbers have been defined manually\n   533\t        if self.allocated_draw is None and self.maximum_draw is None:\n   534\t            utilization = self.get_downstream_powerports().aggregate(\n   535\t                maximum_draw_total=Sum('maximum_draw'),\n   536\t                allocated_draw_total=Sum('allocated_draw'),\n   537\t            )\n   538\t            ret = {\n   539\t                'allocated': utilization['allocated_draw_total'] or 0,\n   540\t                'maximum': utilization['maximum_draw_total'] or 0,\n   541\t                'outlet_count': self.poweroutlets.count(),\n   542\t                'legs': [],\n   543\t            }\n   544\t\n   545\t            # Calculate per-leg aggregates for three-phase power feeds\n   546\t            if len(self.link_peers) == 1 and isinstance(self.link_peers[0], PowerFeed) and \\\n   547\t                    self.link_peers[0].phase == PowerFeedPhaseChoices.PHASE_3PHASE:\n   548\t                for leg, leg_name in PowerOutletFeedLegChoices:\n   549\t                    utilization = allocated_draw_total=Sum('allocated_draw'),\n   552\t                    )\n   553\t                    ret['legs'].append({\n   554\t                        'name': leg_name,\n   555\t 563\t        return {\n   564\t            'allocated': self.allocated_draw or 0,\n   565\t            'maximum': self.maximum_draw or 0,\n   566\t            'outlet_count': self.poweroutlets.count(),\n   567\t            'legs': [],\n   568\t        }\n   569\t\n   570\t\n   571\tclass PowerOutlet(ModularComponentModel, CabledObjectModel, PathEndpoint,   194\t        'weight_unit',\n   195\t    )\n   196\t    prerequisite_models = (\n   197\t        'dcim.Manufacturer',\n   198\t    )\n   199\t\n   200\t    class Meta:\n   201\t        ordering = _('device types')\n   214\t\n   215\t    def __str__(self):\n   216\t        return self.model\n   217\t\n   218\t    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n   219\t        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\n   220\t\n   221\t        # Save a copy of u_height for validation in clean()\n   222\t        self._original_u_height = self.__dict__.get('u_height')\n   223\t\n   224\t        # Save references to the original front/rear images\n   225\t        self._original_front_image = self.__dict__.get('front_image')\n   226\t        self._original_rear_image = self.__dict__.get('rear_image')\n   227\t\n   228\t    @property\n   229\t    def full_name(self):\n   230\t        return f\"{self.manufacturer} {self.model}\"\n   231\t\n   232\t    def to_yaml(self):\n   233\t        data = {\n   234\t            'manufacturer': self.manufacturer.name,\n   235\t            'model': self.model,\n   236\t            'slug': self.slug,\n   237\t            'description': self.description,\n   238\t            'default_platform': self.default_platform.name if self.default_platform else None,\n   239\t            'part_number': self.part_number,\n   240\t            'u_height': float(self.u_height),\n   241\t            'is_full_depth': self.is_full_depth,\n   242\t            'subdevice_role': self.subdevice_role,\n   243\t            'airflow': self.airflow,\n   244\t            'weight': float(self.weight) if self.weight is not None else None,\n   245\t            'weight_unit': self.weight_unit,\n   246\t            'comments': self.comments,\n   247\t        }\n   248\t\n   249\t        # Component templates\n   250\t        if self.consoleporttemplates.exists():\n   251\t            data['console-ports'] = [\n   252\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.consoleporttemplates.all()\n   253\t            ]\n   254\t        if self.consoleserverporttemplates.exists():\n   255\t            data['console-server-ports'] = [\n   256\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.consoleserverporttemplates.all()\n   257\t            ]\n   258\t        if self.powerporttemplates.exists():\n   259\t            data['power-ports'] = [\n   260\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.powerporttemplates.all()\n   261\t            ]\n   262\t        if self.poweroutlettemplates.exists():\n   263\t            data['power-outlets'] = [\n   264\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.poweroutlettemplates.all()\n   265\t            ]\n   266\t        if self.interfacetemplates.exists():\n   267\t            data['interfaces'] = [\n   268\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.interfacetemplates.all()\n   269\t            ]\n   270\t        if self.frontporttemplates.exists():\n   271\t            data['front-ports'] = [\n   272\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.frontporttemplates.all()\n   273\t            ]\n   274\t        if self.rearporttemplates.exists():\n   275\t            data['rear-ports'] = [\n   276\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.rearporttemplates.all()\n   277\t            ]\n   278\t        if self.modulebaytemplates.exists():\n   279\t            data['module-bays'] = [\n   280\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.modulebaytemplates.all()\n   281\t            ]\n   282\t        if self.devicebaytemplates.exists():\n   283\t            data['device-bays'] = [\n   284\t                c.to_yaml() for c in self.devicebaytemplates.all()\n   285\t            ]\n   286\t\n   287\t        return yaml.dump(dict(data), sort_keys=False)\n   288\t\n   289\t    def clean(self):\n   290\t        super().clean()\n   291\t\n   292\t        # U height must be divisible by 0.5\n   293\t        if decimal.Decimal(self.u_height) % decimal.Decimal(0.5):\n   294\t            raise ValidationError({\n   295\t                'u_height': _(\"U height must be in increments of 0.5 rack units.\")\n   296\t            })\n   297\t\n   298\t        # If editing an existing DeviceType to have a larger u_height, first validate that *all* instances of it have\n   299\t        # room to expand within their racks. This validation will impose a very high performance penalty when there are\n   300\t        # many instances to check, but increasing the u_height of a DeviceType should be a very rare occurrence.\n   301\t        if not self._state.adding and self.u_height > self._original_u_height:\n   302\t            for d in Device.objects.filter(device_type=self, position__isnull=False):\n   303\t                face_required = None if self.is_full_depth else d.face\n   304\t                u_available = d.rack.get_available_units(\n   305\t                    u_height=self.u_height,\n   306\t                    rack_face=face_required,\n   307\t                    exclude=[d.pk]\n   308\t                )\n   309\t                if d.position not in u_available:\n   310\t                    raise ValidationError({\n   311\t                        'u_height': _(\n   312\t                            \"Device {device} in rack {rack} does not have sufficient space to accommodate a \"\n   313\t                            \"height of {height}U\"\n   314\t                        ).format(device=d, rack=d.rack, height=self.u_height)\n   315\t                    })\n   316\t\n   317\t        # If modifying the height of an existing DeviceType to 0U, check for any instances assigned to a rack position.\n   318\t        elif not self._state.adding and self._original_u_height > 0 and self.u_height == 0:\n   319\t            racked_instance_count = Device.objects.filter(\n   320\t                device_type=self,\n   321\t                position__isnull=False\n   322\t            ).count()\n   323\t            if racked_instance_count:\n   324\t                url = f\"{reverse('dcim:device_list')}?manufactuer_id={self.manufacturer_id}&device_type_id={self.pk}\"\n   325\t                raise ValidationError({\n   326\t                    'u_height': mark_safe(_(\n   327\t                        'Unable to set 0U height: Found <a href=\"{url}\">{racked_instance_count} instances</a> already '\n   328\t                        'mounted within racks.'\n   329\t                    ).format(url=url, racked_instance_count=racked_instance_count))\n   330\t                })\n   331\t\n   332\t        if (\n   333\t                self.subdevice_role != SubdeviceRoleChoices.ROLE_PARENT\n   334\t        ) and self.pk and self.devicebaytemplates.count():\n   335\t            raise ValidationError({\n   336\t                'subdevice_role': _(\"Must delete all device bay templates associated with this device before \"\n   337\t                                  \"declassifying it as a parent device.\")\n   338\t            })\n   339\t\n   340\t        if self.u_height and self.subdevice_role == SubdeviceRoleChoices.ROLE_CHILD:\n   341\t            raise ValidationError({\n   342\t                'u_height': _(\"Child device types must be 0U.\")\n   343\t            })\n   344\t\n   345\t    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):\n   346\t        ret = super().save(*args, **kwargs)\n   347\t\n   348\t        # Delete any previously uploaded image files that are no longer in use\n   349\t        if self._original_front_image and self.front_image != self._original_front_image:\n   350\t            default_storage.delete(self._original_front_image)\n   351\t        if self._original_rear_image and self.rear_image != self._original_rear_image:\n   352\t            default_storage.delete(self._original_rear_image)\n   353\t\n   354\t        return ret\n   355\t\n   356\t    def delete(self, *args, **kwargs):\n   357\t        super().delete(*args, **kwargs)\n   358\t\n   359\t        # Delete any uploaded image files\n   360\t        if self.front_image:\n   361\t            self.front_image.delete(save=False)\n   362\t        if self.rear_image:\n   363\t            self.rear_image.delete(save=False)\n   364\t\n   365\t    @property\n   366\t    def is_parent_device(self):\n   367\t        return self.subdevice_role == SubdeviceRoleChoices.ROLE_PARENT\n   368\t\n   369\t    @property\n   370\t    def is_child_device(self):\n   371\t        return self.subdevice_role == SubdeviceRoleChoices.ROLE_CHILD\n   372\t\n   373\t\n   374\t#\n   375\t# Devices\n   376\t#\n   377\t\n   378\tclass DeviceRole(NestedGroupModel):\n   379\t    \"\"\"\n   380\t    Devices are organized by functional role; for example, \"Core Switch\" or \"File Server\". Each Device is assigned a DeviceType,\n   498\t    DeviceRole, and (optionally) a Platform. Device names are not required, however if one is set it must be unique.\n   499\t\n   500\t    Each Device must be assigned to a site, and optionally to a rack within that site. Associating a device with a\n   501\t    particular rack face or unit is optional (for example, vertically mounted PDUs do not consume rack units).\n   502\t\n   503\t    When a new Device is created, console/power/interface/device bay components are created along with it as dictated\n   504\t    by the component templates assigned to its DeviceType. __str__(self):\n   271\t        return self.label or self.name.replace('_', ' ').capitalize()\n   272\t\n   273\t    def get_absolute_url(self):\n   274\t        return reverse('extras:customfield', args=[self.pk])\n   275\t\n   276\t    @property\n   277\t    def docs_url(self):\n   278\t        return f'{settings.STATIC_URL}docs/models/extras/customfield/'\n   279\t\n   280\t    def      self._name = self.__dict__.get('name')\n   285\t\n   286\t    @property\n   287\t    def search_type(self):\n   288\t        return SEARCH_TYPES.get(self.type)\n   289\t\n   290\t    @property\n   291\t    def choices(self):\n   292\t        if self.choice_set:\n   293\t            return self.choice_set.choices\n   294\t        return []\n   295\t\n   296\t    def 309\t        Populate initial custom field data upon either a) the creation of a new CustomField, or\n   310\t        b) the assignment of an existing CustomField to new object types.\n   311\t        \"\"\"\n   312\t        if self.default is None:\n   313\t            # We have to convert None to a JSON null for jsonb_set()\n   314\t            value = RawSQL(\"'null'::jsonb\", [])\n   315\t        else:\n   316\t            value = Value(self.default, models.JSONField())\n   317\t        for ct in content_types:\n   318\t            ct.model_class().objects.update(\n   319\t function='jsonb_set'\n   324\t                )\n   325\t            )\n   326\t\n   327\t    def remove_stale_data(self, content_types):\n   328\t        \"\"\"\n   329\t        Delete custom field data which is no longer relevant (either because the CustomField is\n   330\t        no longer assigned to a model, or because it has been deleted).\n   331\t        \"\"\"\n   332\t        for ct in content_types:\n   333\t            if model := ct.model_class():\n   334\t                model.objects.update(\n   335\t                    custom_field_data=F('custom_field_data') - self.name\n   336\t                )\n   337\t\n   338\t    def rename_object_data(self, old_name, new_name):\n   339\t        \"\"\"\n   340\t        Called when a CustomField has been renamed. Removes the original key and inserts the new\n   341\t        one, copying the value of the old key.\n   342\t        \"\"\"\n   343\t        for ct in self.object_types.all():\n   344\t            ct.model_class().objects.update(\n   345\t                custom_field_data=Func(\n   346\t                    F('custom_field_data') - old_name,\n   347\t                    Value([new_name]),\n   348\t                    Func(\n   349\t                        F('custom_field_data'),\n   350\t                        function='jsonb_extract_path_text',\n   351\t                        template=f\"to_jsonb(%(expressions)s -> '{old_name}')\"\n   352\t                    ),\n   353\t                    function='jsonb_set')\n   354\t            )\n   355\t\n   356\t    def clean(self):\n   357\t        super().clean()\n   358\t\n   359\t        # Validate the field's default value (if any)\n   360\t        if self.default is not None:\n   361\t            try:\n   362\t                if self.type in (CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_TEXT, CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_LONGTEXT):\n   363\t                    default_value = str(self.default)\n   364\t                else:\n   365\t                    default_value = self.default\n   366\t                self.validate(default_value)\n   367\t            except ValidationError as err:\n   368\t                raise ValidationError({\n   369\t                    'default': _(\n   370\t                        'Invalid default value \"{value}\": {error}'\n   371\t                    ).format(value=self.default, error=err.message)\n   372\t                })\n   373\t\n   374\t        # Minimum/maximum values can be set only for numeric fields\n   375\t        if self.type not in (CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_INTEGER, CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DECIMAL):\n   376\t            if self.validation_minimum:\n   377\t                raise ValidationError({'validation_minimum': _(\"A minimum value may be set only for numeric fields\")})\n   378\t            if self.validation_maximum:\n   379\t                raise ValidationError({'validation_maximum': _(\"A maximum value may be set only for numeric fields\")})\n   380\t\n   381\t        # Regex validation can be set only for text fields\n   382\t        regex_types = (\n   383\t            CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_TEXT,\n   384\t            CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_LONGTEXT,\n   385\t            CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_URL,\n   386\t        )\n   387\t        if self.validation_regex and self.type not in regex_types:\n   388\t            raise ValidationError({\n   389\t                'validation_regex': _(\"Regular expression validation is supported only for text and URL fields\")\n   390\t            })\n   391\t\n   392\t        # Uniqueness can not be enforced for boolean fields\n   393\t        if self.unique and self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_BOOLEAN:\n   394\t            raise ValidationError({\n   395\t                'unique': _(\"Uniqueness cannot be enforced for boolean fields\")\n   396\t            })\n   397\t\n   398\t        # Choice set must be set on selection fields, and *only* on selection fields\n   399\t        if self.type in (\n   400\t                CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_SELECT,\n   401\t                CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTISELECT\n   402\t        ):\n   403\t            if not self.choice_set:\n   404\t                raise ValidationError({\n   405\t                    'choice_set': _(\"Selection fields must specify a set of choices.\")\n   406\t                })\n   407\t        elif self.choice_set:\n   408\t            raise ValidationError({\n   409\t                'choice_set': _(\"Choices may be set only on selection fields.\")\n   410\t            })\n   411\t\n   412\t        # Object fields must define an object_type; other fields must not\n   413\t        if self.type in (CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_OBJECT, CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTIOBJECT):\n   414\t            if not self.related_object_type:\n   415\t                raise ValidationError({\n   416\t                    'related_object_type': _(\"Object fields must define an object type.\")\n   417\t                })\n   418\t        elif self.related_object_type:\n   419\t            raise ValidationError({\n   420\t                'type': _(\"{type} fields may not define an object type.\") .format(type=self.get_type_display())\n   421\t            })\n   422\t\n   423\t        # Related object filter can be set only for object-type fields, and must contain a dictionary mapping (if set)\n   424\t        if self.related_object_filter is not None:\n   425\t            if self.type not in (CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_OBJECT, CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTIOBJECT):\n   426\t                raise ValidationError({\n   427\t                    'related_object_filter': _(\"A related object filter can be defined only for object fields.\")\n   428\t                })\n   429\t            if type(self.related_object_filter) is not dict:\n   430\t                raise ValidationError({\n   431\t                    'related_object_filter': _(\"Filter must be defined as a dictionary mapping attributes to values.\")\n   432\t                })\n   433\t\n   434\t    def serialize(self, value):\n   435\t        \"\"\"\n   436\t        Prepare a value for storage as JSON data.\n   437\t        \"\"\"\n   438\t        if value is None:\n   439\t            return value\n   440\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATE and type(value) is date:\n   441\t            return value.isoformat()\n   442\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATETIME and type(value) is datetime:\n   443\t            return value.isoformat()\n   444\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_OBJECT:\n   445\t            return value.pk\n   446\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTIOBJECT:\n   447\t            return [obj.pk for obj in value] or None\n   448\t        return value\n   449\t\n   450\t    def deserialize(self, value):\n   451\t        \"\"\"\n   452\t        Convert JSON data to a Python object suitable for the field type.\n   453\t        \"\"\"\n   454\t        if value is None:\n   455\t            return value\n   456\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATE:\n   457\t            try:\n   458\t                return date.fromisoformat(value)\n   459\t            except ValueError:\n   460\t                return value\n   461\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATETIME:\n   462\t            try:\n   463\t                return datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n   464\t            except ValueError:\n   465\t                return value\n   466\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_OBJECT:\n   467\t            model = self.related_object_type.model_class()\n   468\t            return model.objects.filter(pk=value).first()\n   469\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTIOBJECT:\n   470\t            model = self.related_object_type.model_class()\n   471\t            return model.objects.filter(pk__in=value)\n   472\t        return value\n   473\t\n   474\t    def to_form_field(\n   475\t        self,\n   476\t        set_initial=True,\n   477\t        enforce_required=True,\n   478\t        enforce_visibility=True,\n   479\t        for_csv_import=False,\n   480\t        for_filterset_form=False,\n   481\t    ):\n   482\t        \"\"\"\n   483\t        Return a form field suitable for setting a CustomField's value for an object.\n   484\t\n   485\t        set_initial: Set initial data for the field. This should be False when generating a field for bulk editing.\n   486\t        enforce_required: Honor the value of CustomField.required. Set to False for filtering/bulk editing.\n   487\t        enforce_visibility: Honor the value of CustomField.ui_visible. Set to False for filtering.\n   488\t        for_csv_import: Return a form field suitable for bulk import of objects in CSV format.\n   489\t        for_filterset_form: Return a form field suitable for use in a FilterSet form.\n   490\t        \"\"\"\n   491\t        initial = self.default if set_initial else None\n   492\t        required = self.required if enforce_required else False\n   493\t\n   494\t        # Integer\n   495\t        if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_INTEGER:\n   496\t            field = forms.IntegerField(\n   497\t                required=required,\n   498\t                initial=initial,\n   499\t                min_value=self.validation_minimum,\n   500\t                max_value=self.validation_maximum\n   501\t            )\n   502\t\n   503\t        # Decimal\n   504\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DECIMAL:\n   505\t            field = forms.DecimalField(\n   506\t                required=required,\n   507\t                initial=initial,\n   508\t                max_digits=16,\n   509\t                decimal_places=4,\n   510\t                min_value=self.validation_minimum,\n   511\t                max_value=self.validation_maximum\n   512\t            )\n   513\t\n   514\t        # Boolean\n   515\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_BOOLEAN:\n   516\t            choices = (\n   517\t                (None, '---------'),\n   518\t                (True, _('True')),\n   519\t                (False, _('False')),\n   520\t            )\n   521\t            field = forms.NullBooleanField(\n   522\t                required=required, initial=initial, widget=forms.Select(choices=choices)\n   523\t            )\n   524\t\n   525\t        # Date\n   526\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATE:\n   527\t            field = forms.DateField(required=required, initial=initial, widget=DatePicker())\n   528\t\n   529\t        # Date & time\n   530\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATETIME:\n   531\t            field = forms.DateTimeField(required=required, initial=initial, widget=DateTimePicker())\n   532\t\n   533\t        # Select\n   534\t        elif self.type in (CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_SELECT, CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTISELECT):\n   535\t            choices = self.choice_set.choices\n   536\t            default_choice = self.default if self.default in self.choices else None\n   537\t\n   538\t            if not required or default_choice is None:\n   539\t                choices = add_blank_choice(choices)\n   540\t\n   541\t            # Set the initial value to the first available choice (if any)\n   542\t            if set_initial and default_choice:\n   543\t                initial = default_choice\n   544\t\n   545\t            if for_csv_import:\n   546\t                if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_SELECT:\n   547\t                    field_class = CSVChoiceField\n   548\t                else:\n   549\t                    field_class = CSVMultipleChoiceField\n   550\t                field = field_class(choices=choices, required=required, initial=initial)\n   551\t            else:\n   552\t                if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_SELECT and not for_filterset_form:\n   553\t                    field_class = DynamicChoiceField\n   554\t                    widget_class = APISelect\n   555\t                else:\n   556\t                    field_class = DynamicMultipleChoiceField\n   557\t                    widget_class = APISelectMultiple\n   558\t                field = field_class(\n   559\t                    choices=choices,\n   560\t                    required=required,\n   561\t                    initial=initial,\n   562\t                    widget=widget_class(api_url=f'/api/extras/custom-field-choice-sets/{self.choice_set.pk}/choices/')\n   563\t                )\n   564\t\n   565\t        # URL\n   566\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_URL:\n   567\t            field = LaxURLField(assume_scheme='https', required=required, initial=initial)\n   568\t            if self.validation_regex:\n   569\t                field.validators = [\n   570\t                    RegexValidator(\n   571\t                        regex=self.validation_regex,\n   572\t                        message=mark_safe(_(\"Values must match this regex: <code>{regex}</code>\").format(\n   573\t                            regex=escape(self.validation_regex)\n   574\t                        ))\n   575\t                    )\n   576\t                ]\n   577\t\n   578\t        # JSON\n   579\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_JSON:\n   580\t            field = JSONField(required=required, initial=json.dumps(initial) if initial is not None else None)\n   581\t\n   582\t        # Object\n   583\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_OBJECT:\n   584\t            model = self.related_object_type.model_class()\n   585\t            field_class = CSVModelChoiceField if for_csv_import else DynamicModelChoiceField\n   586\t            kwargs = {\n   587\t                'queryset': model.objects.all(),\n   588\t                'required': required,\n   589\t                'initial': initial,\n   590\t            }\n   591\t            if not for_csv_import:\n   592\t                kwargs['query_params'] = self.related_object_filter\n   593\t                kwargs['selector'] = True\n   594\t\n   595\t            field = field_class(**kwargs)\n   596\t\n   597\t        # Multiple objects\n   598\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTIOBJECT:\n   599\t            model = self.related_object_type.model_class()\n   600\t            field_class = CSVModelMultipleChoiceField if for_csv_import else DynamicModelMultipleChoiceField\n   601\t            kwargs = {\n   602\t                'queryset': model.objects.all(),\n   603\t                'required': required,\n   604\t                'initial': initial,\n   605\t            }\n   606\t            if not for_csv_import:\n   607\t                kwargs['query_params'] = self.related_object_filter\n   608\t                kwargs['selector'] = True\n   609\t\n   610\t            field = field_class(**kwargs)\n   611\t\n   612\t        # Text\n   613\t        else:\n   614\t            widget = forms.Textarea if self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_LONGTEXT else None\n   615\t            field = forms.CharField(required=required, initial=initial, widget=widget)\n   616\t            if self.validation_regex:\n   617\t                field.validators = [\n   618\t                    RegexValidator(\n   619\t                        regex=self.validation_regex,\n   620\t                        message=mark_safe(_(\"Values must match this regex: <code>{regex}</code>\").format(\n   621\t                            regex=escape(self.validation_regex)\n   622\t                        ))\n   623\t                    )\n   624\t                ]\n   625\t\n   626\t        field.model = self\n   627\t        field.label = str(self)\n   628\t        if self.description:\n   629\t            field.help_text = render_markdown(self.description)\n   630\t\n   631\t        # Annotate read-only fields\n   632\t        if enforce_visibility and self.ui_editable != CustomFieldUIEditableChoices.YES:\n   633\t            field.disabled = True\n   634\t\n   635\t        return field\n   636\t\n   637\t    def to_filter(self, lookup_expr=None):\n   638\t        \"\"\"\n   639\t        Return a django_filters Filter instance suitable for this field type.\n   640\t\n   641\t        :param lookup_expr: Custom lookup expression (optional)\n   642\t        \"\"\"\n   643\t        kwargs = {\n   644\t            'field_name': f'custom_field_data__{self.name}'\n   645\t        }\n   646\t        # Native numeric filters will use `isnull` by default for empty lookups, but\n   647\t        # JSON fields require `empty` (see bug #20012).\n   648\t        if lookup_expr == 'isnull':\n   649\t            lookup_expr = 'empty'\n   650\t        if lookup_expr is not None:\n   651\t            kwargs['lookup_expr'] = lookup_expr\n   652\t\n   653\t        # 'Empty' lookup is always a boolean\n   654\t        if lookup_expr == 'empty':\n   655\t            filter_class = django_filters.BooleanFilter\n   656\t\n   657\t        # Text/URL\n   658\t        elif self.type in (\n   659\t                CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_TEXT,\n   660\t                CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_LONGTEXT,\n   661\t                CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_URL,\n   662\t        ):\n   663\t            filter_class = filters.MultiValueCharFilter\n   664\t            if self.filter_logic == CustomFieldFilterLogicChoices.FILTER_LOOSE:\n   665\t                kwargs['lookup_expr'] = 'icontains'\n   666\t\n   667\t        # Integer\n   668\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_INTEGER:\n   669\t            filter_class = filters.MultiValueNumberFilter\n   670\t\n   671\t        # Decimal\n   672\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DECIMAL:\n   673\t            filter_class = filters.MultiValueDecimalFilter\n   674\t\n   675\t        # Boolean\n   676\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_BOOLEAN:\n   677\t            filter_class = django_filters.BooleanFilter\n   678\t\n   679\t        # Date\n   680\t        elif self.type == CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_DATE:\n   681\t            filter_class = filters.MultiValueDateFilter\n type=CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTISELECT,\n   383\t            required=False,\n   384\t            choice_set=choice_set\n   385\t        )\n   386\t        cf.object_types.set([self.object_type])\n   387\t        instance = Site.objects.first()\n   388\t        self.assertIsNone(instance.custom_field_data[cf.name])\n   389\t\n   390\t        # Assign a value and check that it is saved\n   391\t        instance.custom_field_data[cf.name] = value\n   392\t        instance.save()\n   393\t        instance.refresh_from_db()\n   394\t        self.assertEqual(instance.custom_field_data[cf.name], value)\n   395\t\n   396\t        # Delete the stored value and check that it is now null\n   397\t        instance.custom_field_data.pop(cf.name)\n   398\t        instance.save()\n   399\t        instance.refresh_from_db()\n   400\t        self.assertIsNone(instance.custom_field_data.get(cf.name))\n   401\t\n   402\t    def test_remove_selected_choice(self):\n   403\t        \"\"\"\n   404\t        Removing a ChoiceSet choice that is referenced by an object should raise\n   405\t        a ValidationError exception.\n   406\t        \"\"\"\n   407\t        CHOICES = (\n   408\t            ('a', 'Option A'),\n   409\t            ('b', 'Option B'),\n   410\t            ('c', 'Option C'),\n   411\t            ('d', 'Option D'),\n   412\t        )\n   413\t\n   414\t        # Create a set of custom field choices\n   415\t        choice_set = CustomFieldChoiceSet.objects.create(\n   416\t            name='Custom Field Choice Set 1',\n   417\t            extra_choices=CHOICES\n   418\t        )\n   419\t\n   420\t        # Create a select custom field\n   421\t        cf = CustomField.objects.create(\n   422\t            name='select_field',\n   423\t            type=CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_SELECT,\n   424\t            required=False,\n   425\t            choice_set=choice_set\n   426\t        )\n   427\t        cf.object_types.set([self.object_type])\n   428\t\n   429\t        # Create a multi-select custom field\n   430\t        cf_multiselect = CustomField.objects.create(\n   431\t            name='multiselect_field',\n   432\t            type=CustomFieldTypeChoices.TYPE_MULTISELECT,\n   433\t            required=False,\n   434\t            choice_set=choice_set\n   435\t        )\n   436\t        cf_multiselect.object_types.set([self.object_type])\n   437\t\n   438\t        # Assign a choice for both custom fields on an object\n   439\t        instance = Site.objects.first()\n   440\t        instance.custom_field_data[cf.name] = 'a'\n   441\t        instance.custom_field_data[cf_multiselect.name] = ['b', 'c']\n   442\t        instance.save()\n   443\t\n   444\t        # Attempting to delete a selected choice should fail\n   445\t        with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):\n   446\t            choice_set.extra_choices = (\n   447\t                ('b', 'Option B'),\n   448\t                ('c', 'Option C'),\n   449\t                ('d', 'Option D'),\n   450\t            )\n   451\t            choice_set.full_clean()\n   452\t\n   453\t        # Attempting to delete either of the multi-select choices should fail\n   454\t        with self.assertRaises(ValidationError):\n   455\t            choice_set.extra_choices = (\n   456\t                ('a', 'Option A'),\n   457\t                ('b', 'Option B'),\n   458\t                ('d', 'Option D'),\n   459\t            )\n   460\t            choice_set.full_clean()\n   461\t\n   462\t        # Removing a non-selected choice should succeed\n   463\t        choice_set.extra_choices = (\n   464\t            ('a', 'Option A'),\n   465\t            ('b', 'Option B'),\n   466\t            ('c', 'Option C'),\n   467\t        )\n   468\t        choice_set.full_clean()\n   469\t\n   470\t    def test_object_field(self):\n   471\t def test_create_single_object_with_defaults(self):\n   878\t        \"\"\"\n   879\t        Create a new site with no specified custom field values and check that it received the default values.\n   880\t        \"\"\"\n   881\t        cf_defaults = {\n   882\t            cf.name: cf.default for cf in CustomField.objects.all()\n   883\t        }\n   884\t        data = {\n   885\t Removed the misleading help text line from the `formfield()` method in `netbox/utilities/fields.py`\n2. Removed the now-unused `mark_safe` import from the file\n\n**Before**:\n```python\ndef formfield(self, **kwargs):\n    kwargs['widget'] = ColorSelect\n    kwargs['help_text'] = mark_safe(_('RGB color in hexadecimal. Example: ') + '<code>00ff00</code>')\n    return super().formfield(**kwargs)\n```\n\n**After**:\n```python\ndef formfield(self, **kwargs):\n    kwargs['widget'] = ColorSelect\n    return super().formfield(**kwargs)\n```\n\nThe `ColorSelect` widget is a dropdown picker (as shown in `netbox/utilities/forms/widgets/select.py`), so users select from predefined color choices rather than typing hex codes manually. The removed help text was therefore incorrect and misleading."}}}}}

This will typically be called automatically\n   107\t        by ChangeLoggingMiddleware.\n   108\t        \"\"\"\n   109\t        # TODO: Fix circular import\n   110\t        from core.models import ObjectChange\n   111\t\n   112\t        exclude = []\n   113\t        if get_config().CHANGELOG_SKIP_EMPTY_CHANGES:\n   114\t            exclude = ['last_updated']\n   115\t\n   116\t        objectchange = ObjectChange(\n   117\t            changed_object=self,\n   118\t            object_repr=str(self)[:200],\n   119\t            action=action,\n   120\t            message=self._changelog_message or '',\n   121\t        )\n   122\t        if hasattr(self, '_prechange_snapshot'):\n   123\t            objectchange.prechange_data = self._prechange_snapshot\n   124\t        if action in (ObjectChangeActionChoices.ACTION_CREATE, ObjectChangeActionChoices.ACTION_UPDATE):\n   125\t            self._postchange_snapshot = self.serialize_object(exclude=exclude)\n   126\t            objectchange.postchange_data = self._postchange_snapshot\n   127\t\n   128\t        return objectchange\n   129\t    to_objectchange.alters_data = True\n   130\t\n   131\t\n   132\tclass CloningMixin(models.Model):\n   133\t    \"\"\"\n   134\t    Provides the clone() method used to prepare a copy of existing objects.\n   135\t    \"\"\"\n   136\t    class Meta:\n   137\t        abstract = True\n   138\t\n   139\t    def clone(self):\n   140\t        \"\"\"\n   141\t        Returns a dictionary of attributes suitable for creating a copy of the current instance. This is used for pre-\n   142\t        populating an object creation form in the UI. By default, this method will replicate any fields listed in the\n   143\t        model's `clone_fields` list (if defined), but it can be overridden to apply custom logic.\n   144\t\n   145\t        ```python\n   146\t        class MyModel(NetBoxModel):\n   147\t            def clone(self):\n   148\t                attrs = super().clone()\n   149\t                attrs['extra-value'] = 123\n   150\t                return attrs\n   151\t        ```\n   152\t        \"\"\"\n   153\t        attrs = {}\n   154\t\n   155\t        for field_name in getattr(self, 'clone_fields', []):\n   156\t            field = self._meta.get_field(field_name)\n   157\t            field_value = field.value_from_object(self)\n   158\t            if field_value and isinstance(field, models.ManyToManyField):\n   159\t                attrs[field_name] = [v.pk for v in field_value]\n   160\t            elif field_value and isinstance(field, models.JSONField):\n   161\t                attrs[field_name] = json.dumps(field_value)\n   162\t            elif field_value not in (None, ''):\n   163\t                attrs[field_name] = field_value\n   164\t\n   165\t        # Handle GenericForeignKeys. If the CT and ID fields are being cloned, also\n   166\t        # include the name of the GFK attribute itself, as this is what forms expect.\n   167\t        for field in self._meta.private_fields:\n   168\t            if isinstance(field, GenericForeignKey):\n   169\t                if field.ct_field in attrs and field.fk_field in attrs:\n   170\t                    attrs[field.name] = attrs[field.fk_field]\n   171\t\n   172\t        # Include tags (if applicable)\n   173\t        if is_taggable(self):\n   174\t            attrs['tags'] = [tag.pk for tag in self.tags.all()]\n   175\t\n   176\t        # Include any cloneable custom fields\n   177\t        if hasattr(self, 'custom_fields'):\n   178\t            for field in self.custom_fields:\n   179\t                if field.is_cloneable:\n   180\t                    attrs[f'cf_{field.name}'] = self.custom_field_data.get(field.name)\n   181\t\n   182\t        return attrs\n   183\t\n   184\t\n   185\tclass The broken module would persist in the database due to a uniqueness constraint, preventing re-upload of a corrected script with the same filename.\n\n- **After**: The script content is validated during the upload process (in both the web form and API). If the script has syntax errors, import failures, or other runtime errors, a `ValidationError` is raised with a descriptive message, and the file is never saved to storage or the database entry is never created. This allows users to fix their script and retry the upload."}}}}}

Here's a summary:\n\n## Changes Made\n\n### 1. `netbox/tenancy/models/contacts.py`\n- Added `SQCountDistinct` class - A custom Subquery that uses `COUNT(DISTINCT ...)` to avoid double-counting contacts\n- Added `ContactGroupManager` class - A custom TreeManager with `annotate_contacts()` method that:\n  - Uses a subquery to find all contact-group relationships within the MPTT hierarchy (current group + all descendants)\n  - Counts DISTINCT contact_ids to avoid double-counting contacts that belong to multiple child groups\n- Modified `ContactGroup` model to use `ContactGroupManager()` instead of the default TreeManager\n\n### 2. `netbox/tenancy/views.py`\n- `ContactGroupListView`: Changed from `add_related_count()` to `annotate_contacts()`\n- `ContactGroupBulkEditView`: Changed from `add_related_count()` to `annotate_contacts()`\n- `ContactGroupBulkDeleteView`: Changed from `add_related_count()` to `annotate_contacts()`\n- `ContactGroupView.get_extra_context()`: Added `.distinct()` to the contacts queryset to prevent double-counting in the detail view\n\n### 3. `netbox/tenancy/api/views.py`\n- `ContactGroupViewSet`: Changed from `add_related_count()` to `annotate_contacts()`\n\n## How the Fix Works\n\nThe original bug was caused by django-mptt's `add_related_count()` which uses a simple subquery count (`COUNT(*)`) that doesn't deduplicate across the ManyToMany relationship. When a contact belongs to multiple child groups, it would be counted multiple times.\n\nThe fix uses a custom `SQCountDistinct` subquery with `COUNT(DISTINCT contact_id)` which correctly deduplicates contacts that belong to multiple child groups within the same parent hierarchy. This ensures that each contact is counted only once per parent group, regardless of how many child groups they belong to."}}}}}
